name: Laughlin AFB
market_label: Del Rio / Val Verde County Market
profile: Military-friendly Laughlin AFB housing snapshot anchored to Del Rio and Val Verde County, built for PCSUnited base-level market overviews.

market_metrics.market_type_summary: Affordable small military training market with low commute pressure, slower resale velocity, and high assignment-length sensitivity

mortgage_assumptions.defaults_note: Based on April 2026 Del Rio VA loan planning assumptions.

notes: Texas property taxes materially affect true monthly ownership cost even in affordable Del Rio price bands.
Laughlin buyers should compare mortgage, property tax, insurance, utilities, commute, property condition, and assignment length together.
Lower purchase prices can make ownership attractive, but smaller-market resale depth and training assignment timelines must be considered.

avg_home_mortgage_monthly.notes: VA loan estimate for Del Rio / Laughlin AFB area

crime_status.installation: Extremely safe; gated military access with controlled entry and 24/7 base security.
crime_status.metro_del_rio_val_verde: Del Rio is a small border-region market where safety, property condition, neighborhood fit, school assignment, and resale depth should be reviewed by exact address.

school_quality.on_base: No full on-base K-12 school system should be assumed; families should verify current school options and attendance boundaries by exact housing address.
school_quality.off_base: Del Rio and Val Verde County school options vary by exact address. Families should verify school assignment, commute, and support services before choosing housing.

rental_vacancy.notes: Rental inventory is influenced by Laughlin pilot training turnover, small-market supply, border-region demand, and limited family-size rental depth.

rental_metrics.notes: Based on 3-bedroom military-family rental planning estimates across Del Rio and nearby Laughlin commute corridors.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Pilot-training-focused Air Force community with low commute stress, affordable housing, tight base-town relationship, and strong military visibility in Del Rio.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Del Rio offers one of the more accessible Air Force purchase-price environments, but buyers must account for Texas property taxes, smaller-market resale depth, and whether the assignment timeline supports ownership.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest for disciplined VA buyers targeting clean, well-located Del Rio homes with practical commute, strong condition, and clear resale logic.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Laughlin AFB gives military families strong affordability and very low commute pressure, but the best housing move depends on assignment length, resale depth, property condition, taxes, and whether Del Rio lifestyle fits the household.

summary_points: Laughlin AFB sits just outside Del Rio, one of the more affordable Air Force housing markets by purchase price.
Low commute pressure is a major lifestyle advantage for pilot training families and permanent-party personnel.
The Del Rio market can be attractive for VA buyers, but resale depth is thinner than larger metros.
Texas property taxes and insurance still shape the true monthly ownership cost.
The best Laughlin housing decision balances BAH, property taxes, assignment length, family needs, school fit, property condition, and resale flexibility.
opportunities: Lower purchase prices than most Air Force markets.
Short commute options make daily life simpler for many Laughlin personnel.
Military-connected demand supports rental relevance near Del Rio.
Clean 3-bedroom homes can provide practical ownership and rental value.
Lake Amistad and small-town lifestyle can appeal to users who value outdoor access and slower pace.
risks: Smaller-market resale depth can increase risk for short assignments.
Texas property taxes can erode affordability if buyers only focus on purchase price.
Spouse employment options may be more limited than in larger metros.
Older homes may require roof, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, foundation, or insulation review.
Del Rio lifestyle may not fit every family, especially users needing big-city amenities.
buyer_notes: Laughlin buyers should compare Del Rio neighborhoods by commute, school assignment, condition, price, property taxes, and resale logic.
Do not buy only because the price is low; assignment length and exit strategy matter heavily in this smaller market.
A clean, well-maintained home with strong HVAC, roof, foundation, and resale appeal may be better than the cheapest available home.
Renting can be smart for short training timelines or users unsure about Del Rio lifestyle fit.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who preserve monthly flexibility and avoid over-improving for the local resale pool.
seller_notes: Sellers should position homes around Laughlin commute, VA readiness, condition, roof/HVAC quality, tax transparency, and practical monthly value.
Move-in-ready homes with clean inspection history and PCS-friendly timing can stand out.
Military buyers respond well to realistic payment logic and clear maintenance history.
Homes in strong Del Rio pockets should emphasize low commute, base access, family practicality, and resale usability.
investor_angles: Military training turnover and Del Rio workforce demand support rental relevance, but underwriting must be conservative.
Cash flow can work because acquisition prices are low compared with larger military metros.
Investors should stress-test vacancy, repairs, property taxes, HVAC replacement, tenant turnover, and smaller-market resale risk.
Clean single-family homes in practical commute corridors may outperform cheaper properties with deferred maintenance.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in payment, not just list price, as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, utilities, commute, property condition, and school quality before locking a neighborhood.
This market rewards disciplined buyers more than emotional stretch buyers.
A lower purchase price can materially improve monthly flexibility, but only if the home has realistic resale and maintenance profile.
seller_guidance: Price against current inventory and days on market, not older peak assumptions.
Condition and presentation matter more when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent demand can be a strength if framed correctly with commute, condition, and monthly value.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Laughlin AFB and Del Rio commute corridors.
Investors should still model vacancy, tax drag, insurance, HVAC, and maintenance conservatively.
A good rental here is about stable spread, clean condition, and practical base access — not just headline affordability.

market.market_type_summary: Affordable small military training market with slower resale velocity and low commute pressure

base_profile.metro: Del Rio / Val Verde County, TX
base_profile.market_label: Del Rio / Laughlin Military Housing Market
base_profile.installation_type: Pilot Training / Undergraduate Pilot Training Base
base_profile.host_or_major_command: 47th Flying Training Wing / Air Education and Training Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Laughlin AFB is a major Air Education and Training Command undergraduate pilot training installation near Del Rio, Texas. The base supports pilot training, flying operations, instructor cadre, student throughput, and mission support functions tied closely to the Del Rio community.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Pilot-training-focused, small-market, affordable, commute-light, assignment-length-sensitive, family-relevant, and highly tied to Del Rio housing quality, BAH fit, school options, and resale depth.
base_profile.base_bluf: Laughlin is not just a Del Rio housing decision. It is a training-timeline, BAH, property-tax, resale-depth, spouse-employment, school-zone, and small-market lifestyle decision. The low prices can help, but the wrong home can still create exit risk.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize assignment length, school-zone verification, BAH fit, property condition, resale logic, and commute testing before signing a lease or purchase contract.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, roommate/rental flexibility, and access to food, gyms, Lake Amistad, and daily services.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: The strongest Laughlin housing conversations are about training timeline, BAH realism, Del Rio lifestyle fit, resale risk, and property condition — not generic Texas market stats.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Laughlin is influenced by pilot training volume, instructor assignments, PCS movement, student turnover, and the limited depth of clean Del Rio rental inventory.

Users wanting to reduce small-market rental uncertainty

on_base_housing.pcsu_strategy_note: Compare on-base housing against off-base rent and mortgage options. The best choice depends on rank, dependents, BAH, commute tolerance, school priorities, training timeline, small-market resale risk, pet needs, and how long the assignment may last.

commute_intelligence.commute_bluf: For Laughlin, commute risk is usually low if the user lives in Del Rio, but gate hours, assignment schedule, heat, distance from services, and smaller-market housing quality still matter.
primary_commute_factors: Exact duty location on or near the installation
Pilot training schedule or early report time

commute_bands.label: Close-in
commute_bands.fit: Best for users prioritizing proximity and daily convenience
commute_bands.watchout: Close-in areas still need property, school, and condition screening.

commute_bands.label: Balanced
commute_bands.fit: Often the best balance between housing quality, services, and commute
commute_bands.watchout: Gate choice and exact home condition can still change the real answer.

commute_bands.label: Lifestyle tradeoff
commute_bands.fit: May work for users wanting space, Lake Amistad access, or quieter areas
commute_bands.watchout: Can become inconvenient for early reporting, frequent base errands, or family needs.

commute_bands.label: Extended commute
commute_bands.fit: Only for users with strong household reasons to live farther from Del Rio
commute_bands.watchout: Longer commute can overpower lower price or lifestyle appeal.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Use West Gate as the primary 24/7 gate assumption.
Check North Gate as an optional commute helper only when the user's route and schedule fit posted hours.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on price.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, users should test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window they would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Laughlin-area housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, property taxes, utilities, debt, and assignment length. BAH may support buying comfortably in Del Rio, but short training timelines and smaller-market resale risk can make renting or on-base housing smarter for some users.
recommended_calculations: BAH with dependents vs estimated rent
BAH without dependents vs estimated rent
Total housing cost vs 30% income lane
Residual monthly income after expenses and housing
bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter, not a promise of affordability. The Base tab should push users into the PCSUnited financial calculator when the housing gap, assignment length, or resale risk looks tight.

family_readiness.family_bluf: Families PCSing to Laughlin should verify school zones, childcare options, medical access, spouse employment, Del Rio lifestyle fit, and housing availability before choosing a neighborhood.

school_guidance.school_zone_warning: Always verify school assignment by exact address before signing a lease or purchase contract.
school_guidance.nearby_school_district_note: Common Laughlin-area school research will usually focus on San Felipe Del Rio CISD and address-specific school assignments in Del Rio and Val Verde County.
school_guidance.school_liaison_note: Use official school liaison resources where available for military school transition support.

childcare_guidance.cdc_note: Child Development Center availability, waitlists, and eligibility should be verified directly with official base resources.
childcare_guidance.youth_program_note: Youth programs and school-age care can materially affect family housing decisions.

medical_guidance.medical_warning: Verify clinic availability, pharmacy procedures, and emergency care options directly with official medical sources before relying on any single facility.

family_readiness.spouse_employment_note: Del Rio is a smaller employment market than most Air Force metros, so spouse employment, remote work, childcare, and commute should be considered together.

before_arrival: Verify official gate and Visitor Control Center hours.
Confirm sponsor contact and unit reporting instructions.
Confirm lodging or temporary housing plan.
Review housing office guidance before signing any lease or purchase contract.
Compare BAH against rent, mortgage, taxes, insurance, utilities, and debt.
Shortlist neighborhoods by likely gate and commute band.
Verify school zones by exact property address.
Check pet policies for on-base housing, rentals, and temporary lodging.
first_week: Visit or contact the housing office if still deciding between on-base and off-base options.
Drive the commute from target neighborhoods during expected report time.
Tour properties before committing when possible.
Avoid choosing purely by low rent or low purchase price.
first_30_days: Finalize housing only after comparing commute, BAH fit, school zones, property condition, resale risk, and total monthly cost.
Save emergency, medical, gate, and housing office contact information.
Update PCSUnited profile and financial readiness dashboard with final housing numbers.
Review whether the selected area supports the user's assignment length and family needs.

pcs_watchouts.title: Gate hours can change
pcs_watchouts.message: Use official Laughlin gate pages before travel or commute planning.

pcs_watchouts.title: Assignment length matters heavily
pcs_watchouts.message: Short training timelines can make renting or on-base housing safer than buying, even when purchase prices look attractive.

pcs_watchouts.title: Distance is not the full decision
pcs_watchouts.message: Neighborhoods should be compared by realistic gate access, school fit, property condition, and resale depth.

pcs_watchouts.title: School zones are address-specific
pcs_watchouts.message: Never assume a school assignment from neighborhood name alone.

pcs_watchouts.title: BAH is not the full affordability picture
pcs_watchouts.message: Users must compare rent or mortgage against income, debt, expenses, utilities, taxes, insurance, and savings goals.

pcs_watchouts.title: Small-market resale risk is real
pcs_watchouts.message: A home that is easy to buy may not be equally easy to sell if condition, location, or timing is weak.

pcs_watchouts.title: Heat and utilities matter
pcs_watchouts.message: Summer cooling costs, HVAC age, insulation, and utility reliability should be reviewed before calling a home affordable.

pcs_watchouts.title: On-base housing is not automatically the best financial answer
pcs_watchouts.message: Compare waitlist, BAH capture, commute, home condition, schools, assignment length, and lifestyle before deciding.

listing_angles: Easy access to US-90 and Lake Amistad
common_objections: Affordability gap between BAH and mortgage
recommended_agent_behavior: Always recommend school-zone verification by address.
Use payment math, not just listing price.

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, market prices, insurance estimates, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

demographics.provider: US Census Bureau / Del Rio and Val Verde County regional planning estimates

rent.provider: Local rental listing estimates / Del Rio rental observations

home_price.provider: Zillow / Del Rio and Val Verde County housing market observations

parser_notes: This Laughlin AFB file follows the same tempo and structure as the uploaded Lackland.json reference, including market metrics, costs, ownership notes, rental metrics, bedroom bands, housing market object, base_profile, gates, major services, on-base housing, recommended neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, family readiness, PCS watchouts, realtor intelligence, data confidence, source notes, and compatibility notes.
Base-facing label is set to Laughlin AFB, while Del Rio is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.
This file includes the shared placeholder base_image_url requested for bases without finished map images.
Gate and housing office details are aligned to official Laughlin AFB and Air Force Housing public sources as of June 27, 2026.
